network.c(3) Library Functions Manual network.c(3)

NAME

network.c - 🌐 Cross-platform socket I/O with timeout management and connection handling

SYNOPSIS

Functions


ssize_t send_with_timeout (socket_t sockfd, const void *data, size_t len, uint64_t timeout_ns)
Send data with timeout using chunked transmission. ssize_t recv_with_timeout (socket_t sockfd, void *buf, size_t len, uint64_t timeout_ns)
Receive data with timeout. int accept_with_timeout (socket_t listenfd, struct sockaddr *addr, socklen_t *addrlen, uint64_t timeout_ns)
Accept connection with timeout. asciichat_error_t set_socket_timeout (socket_t sockfd, uint64_t timeout_ns)
Set socket timeout. asciichat_error_t set_socket_keepalive (socket_t sockfd)
Set socket keepalive. asciichat_error_t set_socket_nonblocking (socket_t sockfd)
Set socket non-blocking. asciichat_error_t socket_configure_buffers (socket_t sockfd)
Configure socket buffers and TCP_NODELAY for optimal performance. const char * network_error_string ()
Get human-readable error string for network errors. bool connect_with_timeout (socket_t sockfd, const struct sockaddr *addr, socklen_t addrlen, int timeout_seconds)
Connect with timeout.

Detailed Description

🌐 Cross-platform socket I/O with timeout management and connection handling

Definition in file network/network.c.

Function Documentation

int accept_with_timeout (socket_t listenfd, struct sockaddr * addr, socklen_t * addrlen, uint64_t timeout_ns)

Accept connection with timeout.

Parameters

listenfd Listening socket
addr Client address structure
addrlen Length of address structure
timeout_seconds Timeout in seconds

Returns

Client socket, or -1 on error

Definition at line 251 of file network/network.c.

251                                                                                                            {
252   // Set up poll for accept timeout (in nanoseconds)
253   struct pollfd pfd;
254   pfd.fd = listenfd;
255   pfd.events = POLLIN;
256   pfd.revents = 0;
257 
258   int result = socket_poll(&pfd, 1, (int64_t)timeout_ns);
259 
260   if (result <= 0) {
261     if (result == 0) {
262       // Timeout is expected behavior for server waiting for connections
263       asciichat_errno = ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
264       asciichat_errno_context.code = ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
265       asciichat_errno_context.has_system_error = true;
266       asciichat_errno_context.system_errno = ETIMEDOUT;
267       return -1;
268     }
269 
270     if (network_handle_select_error(result)) {
271       return -1; // Don't retry for accept
272     }
273     return -1;
274   }
275 
276   // Check if socket is ready
277   if (!(pfd.revents & POLLIN)) {
278     asciichat_errno = ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
279     asciichat_errno_context.code = ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
280     asciichat_errno_context.has_system_error = true;
281     asciichat_errno_context.system_errno = ETIMEDOUT;
282     return -1;
283   }
284 
285   // Check if socket is still valid before attempting accept
286   if (listenfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
287     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "accept_with_timeout: listening socket is closed");
288     return -1;
289   }
290 
291   socket_t accept_result = socket_accept(listenfd, addr, addrlen, "connection");
292 
293   if (accept_result == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
294     // Check if this is a socket closed error (common during shutdown)
295     int error_code = socket_get_last_error();
296 
297     if (socket_is_invalid_socket_error(error_code)) {
298       // During shutdown, don't log this as an error since it's expected behavior
299       asciichat_errno = ERROR_NETWORK;
300       asciichat_errno_context.code = ERROR_NETWORK;
301       asciichat_errno_context.has_system_error = false;
302     } else {
303       SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "accept_with_timeout accept failed: %s", socket_get_error_string());
304     }
305     return -1;
306   }
307 
308   return (int)accept_result;
309 }




References asciichat_errno, asciichat_errno_context,
    error_code, and SET_ERRNO.






bool connect_with_timeout (socket_t sockfd, const
  struct sockaddr * addr, socklen_t addrlen, int timeout_seconds)


Connect with timeout.


Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


addr Address to connect to


addrlen Address length


timeout_seconds Timeout in seconds


Returns


true on success, false on failure


Definition at line 442 of file
  network/network.c.


442                                                                                                                 {
443   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
444     errno = EBADF;
445     return false;
446   }
447 
448   // Set socket to non-blocking for timeout control
449   if (set_socket_nonblocking(sockfd) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
450     return false;
451   }
452 
453   // Attempt connection
454   int result = connect(sockfd, addr, addrlen);
455 
456   if (result == 0) {
457     // Connected immediately
458     return true;
459   }
460 
461   // Check if connection is in progress (expected for non-blocking sockets)
462   int error = socket_get_last_error();
463   if (!socket_is_in_progress_error(error) && !socket_is_would_block_error(error)) {
464     return false;
465   }
466 
467   // Use select to wait for connection with timeout
468   fd_set write_fds;
469   struct timeval timeout;
470 
471   socket_fd_zero(&write_fds);
472   socket_fd_set(sockfd, &write_fds);
473 
474   timeout.tv_sec = timeout_seconds;
475   timeout.tv_usec = 0;
476 
477   result = socket_select(sockfd, NULL, &write_fds, NULL, &timeout);
478 
479   if (result <= 0) {
480     return false; // Timeout or error
481   }
482 
483   if (!socket_fd_isset(sockfd, &write_fds)) {
484     return false; // Socket not ready
485   }
486 
487   // Check if connection was successful
488   int error_code = 0;
489   socklen_t error_len = sizeof(error_code);
490 
491   if (socket_getsockopt(sockfd, SOL_SOCKET, SO_ERROR, &error_code, &error_len) != 0) {
492     return false;
493   }
494 
495   if (error_code != 0) {
496     return false;
497   }
498 
499   // Connection successful - restore blocking mode
500   if (socket_set_blocking(sockfd) != 0) {
501     log_warn("Failed to restore socket to blocking mode after connect");
502     // Continue anyway - non-blocking mode works but may cause issues with send/recv
503   }
504 
505   return true;
506 }






References error_code, and
  set_socket_nonblocking().

    tcp_client_connect().

ssize_t
  recv_with_timeout (socket_t sockfd, void * buf, size_t len, uint64_t
  timeout_ns)


Receive data with timeout.


Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


buf Buffer to receive data


len Length of buffer


timeout_seconds Timeout in seconds


Returns


Number of bytes received, or -1 on error


Definition at line 182 of file
  network/network.c.


182                                                                                        {
183   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
184     errno = EBADF;
185     return -1;
186   }
187 
188   ssize_t total_received = 0;
189   char *data = (char *)buf;
190 
191   while (total_received < (ssize_t)len) {
192     // Set up poll for read timeout (in nanoseconds)
193     struct pollfd pfd;
194     pfd.fd = sockfd;
195     pfd.events = POLLIN;
196     pfd.revents = 0;
197 
198     // Use provided timeout for recv() calls - don't artificially limit data packets in test mode
199     // Timeouts should only apply to initial handshake, not to receiving data on active connections
200     int result = socket_poll(&pfd, 1, (int64_t)timeout_ns);
201     if (result <= 0) {
202       if (result == 0) {
203         SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, "recv_with_timeout timed out after %llu nanoseconds",
204                       (unsigned long long)timeout_ns);
205         return -1;
206       }
207       if (network_handle_select_error(result)) {
208         continue; // Retry
209       }
210       return -1; // Fatal error
211     }
212 
213     // Check if socket is ready
214     if (!(pfd.revents & POLLIN)) {
215       SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, "recv_with_timeout socket not ready after poll");
216       return -1;
217     }
218 
219     // Calculate how much we still need to receive
220     size_t bytes_to_recv = len - (size_t)total_received;
221     ssize_t received = socket_recv(sockfd, data + total_received, bytes_to_recv, 0);
222 
223     if (received < 0) {
224       int error = errno;
225       if (network_handle_recv_error(error)) {
226         continue; // Retry
227       }
228       return -1; // Fatal error
229     }
230 
231     if (received == 0) {
232       // Connection closed by peer
233       log_debug("Connection closed by peer during recv");
234       return total_received; // Return what we got so far
235     }
236 
237     total_received += received;
238   }
239 
240   return total_received;
241 }






Referenced by packet_receive(), and
    receive_packet_secure().






ssize_t
  send_with_timeout (socket_t sockfd, const void * data, size_t len,
  uint64_t timeout_ns)


Send data with timeout using chunked transmission.


Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


data Data to send


len Length of data


timeout_seconds Timeout in seconds


Returns


Number of bytes sent, or -1 on error


Definition at line 112 of file
  network/network.c.


112                                                                                               {
113   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
114     errno = EBADF;
115     return -1;
116   }
117 
118   size_t total_sent = 0;
119   const char *data_ptr = (const char *)data;
120 
121   while (total_sent < len) {
122     // Calculate chunk size
123     size_t bytes_to_send = len - total_sent;
124     const size_t MAX_CHUNK_SIZE = 65536; // 64KB chunks for reliable TCP transmission
125     if (bytes_to_send > MAX_CHUNK_SIZE) {
126       bytes_to_send = MAX_CHUNK_SIZE;
127     }
128 
129     // Set up poll for write timeout (in nanoseconds)
130     struct pollfd pfd;
131     pfd.fd = sockfd;
132     pfd.events = POLLOUT;
133     pfd.revents = 0;
134 
135     int64_t effective_timeout_ns = network_is_test_environment() ? (100LL * NS_PER_MS_INT) : (int64_t)timeout_ns;
136     int result = socket_poll(&pfd, 1, effective_timeout_ns);
137     if (result <= 0) {
138       if (result == 0) {
139         SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, "send_with_timeout timed out after %llu nanoseconds",
140                       (unsigned long long)timeout_ns);
141         return -1;
142       }
143       if (network_handle_select_error(result)) {
144         continue; // Retry
145       }
146       return -1; // Fatal error
147     }
148 
149     // Check if socket is ready for writing
150     if (!(pfd.revents & POLLOUT)) {
151       SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, "send_with_timeout socket not ready for writing after poll");
152       return -1;
153     }
154 
155     // Use platform-specific send
156     ssize_t sent = socket_send(sockfd, data_ptr + total_sent, bytes_to_send, 0);
157 
158     if (sent < 0) {
159       int error = errno;
160       if (network_handle_send_error(error)) {
161         continue; // Retry
162       }
163       return -1; // Fatal error
164     }
165 
166     if (sent > 0) {
167       total_sent += (size_t)sent;
168     }
169   }
170 
171   return (ssize_t)total_sent;
172 }






Referenced by packet_send().






asciichat_error_t
  set_socket_keepalive (socket_t sockfd)


Set socket keepalive.


Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 356 of file
  network/network.c.


356                                                         {
357   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
358     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid socket file descriptor");
359   }
360   int result = socket_set_keepalive_params(sockfd, true, KEEPALIVE_IDLE, KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL, KEEPALIVE_COUNT);
361   if (result != 0) {
362     return SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to set socket keepalive parameters");
363   }
364   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
365 }

375   }
376   int result = socket_set_nonblocking(sockfd, true);

References SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by connect_with_timeout().






asciichat_error_t
  set_socket_timeout (socket_t sockfd, uint64_t timeout_ns)


Set socket timeout.


Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


timeout_ns Timeout in nanoseconds (converted to milliseconds for socket
  level)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Sets socket-level timeouts (SO_RCVTIMEO/SO_SNDTIMEO) as a safety
    net fallback. Actual precision depends on platform: milliseconds on most
    systems, microseconds on some. Works in conjunction with application-level
    timeouts via socket_poll.


Definition at line 321 of file
  network/network.c.


321                                                                            {
322   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
323     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid socket file descriptor");
324   }
325 
326   // Convert nanoseconds to milliseconds for socket-level timeout
327   // Note: socket-level timeouts have ~millisecond granularity on most platforms
328   uint64_t timeout_ms = timeout_ns / (1000 * 1000);
329   if (timeout_ms == 0 && timeout_ns > 0) {
330     timeout_ms = 1; // Ensure at least 1ms for non-zero timeouts
331   }
332 
333   // Set both receive and send timeouts using struct timeval
334   struct timeval tv;
335   tv.tv_sec = (time_t)(timeout_ms / 1000);
336   tv.tv_usec = (long)((timeout_ms % 1000) * 1000);
337 
338   // Set receive timeout
339   if (socket_setsockopt(sockfd, SOL_SOCKET, SO_RCVTIMEO, &tv, sizeof(tv)) != 0) {
340     return SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to set socket receive timeout");
341   }
342 
343   // Set send timeout
344   if (socket_setsockopt(sockfd, SOL_SOCKET, SO_SNDTIMEO, &tv, sizeof(tv)) != 0) {
345     return SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to set socket send timeout");
346   }
347 
348   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
349 }






References SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by add_client().






asciichat_error_t
  socket_configure_buffers (socket_t sockfd)


Configure socket buffers and TCP_NODELAY for optimal
  performance.


Parameters


sockfd Socket file descriptor


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, ERROR_NETWORK_CONFIG on
  failure


Definition at line 388 of file
  network/network.c.


388                                                             {
389   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
390     return SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_NETWORK, "Invalid socket file descriptor");
391   }
392 
393   int failed_options = 0;
394 
395   // Attempt to configure 1MB send buffer for optimal frame transmission
396   int send_buffer_size = 1024 * 1024;
397   if (socket_setsockopt(sockfd, SOL_SOCKET, SO_SNDBUF, &send_buffer_size, sizeof(send_buffer_size)) < 0) {
398     log_warn("Failed to set send buffer size to 1MB: %s", network_error_string());
399     failed_options++;
400   }
401 
402   // Attempt to configure 1MB receive buffer for optimal frame reception
403   int recv_buffer_size = 1024 * 1024;
404   if (socket_setsockopt(sockfd, SOL_SOCKET, SO_RCVBUF, &recv_buffer_size, sizeof(recv_buffer_size)) < 0) {
405     log_warn("Failed to set receive buffer size to 1MB: %s", network_error_string());
406     failed_options++;
407   }
408 
409   // Attempt to enable TCP_NODELAY to disable Nagle's algorithm for low-latency transmission
410   // This must be set even if buffer configuration fails, as it's essential for real-time video.
411   int tcp_nodelay = 1;
412   if (socket_setsockopt(sockfd, IPPROTO_TCP, TCP_NODELAY, &tcp_nodelay, sizeof(tcp_nodelay)) < 0) {
413     log_warn("Failed to set TCP_NODELAY: %s", network_error_string());
414     failed_options++;
415   }
416 
417   // Return error only if ALL options failed
418   if (failed_options >= 3) {
419     return SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to configure all socket options");
420   }
421 
422   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
423 }

    tcp_client_connect().
